steps.title: Collect 100-200 staged embryos in a 1
steps.instruction: Collect 100-200 staged embryos in a 1.5 ml eppendorf tube. Wash with PBT twice. Either centrifuge them at a low speed (<= 500 g) for a minute or let them precipitate by themselves.

steps.title: Remove all the liquid, add 50 µL of Trizol
steps.instruction: Remove all the liquid, add 50 µL of Trizol. Homogenize the embryos using a plastic pestle. Depending on the amount of material, add 50-150 µL of Trizol trying to wash the pestle. The protocol proceedes with the assumption that the total volume of Trizol is now 200 µL . Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 1/5 volume of chloroform (for...
steps.instruction: Add 1/5 volume of chloroform (for 200 µL of Trizol it would be 40 µL of chloroform). Vortex for 00:00:30 and incubate at RT for 00:03:00

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:15:00 at 12000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:15:00 at 12000 x g, °C, at 4 °C

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase to a new tube and add 0.8-1 volume of isopropanol. Incubate for 00:10:00 at RT if expected yield is high, or at -20 °C for 02:00:00 if you want RNA to precipitate better.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and wash the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and wash the pellet one time with 200 µL of cold 70% EtOH. Try to dislodge the pellet from the tube side but do not suck it into the pipette tip when mixing.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:10:00 at 12000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:10:00 at 12000 x g, °C, at 4 °C

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 10000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 10000 x g, °C, at 4 °C . Discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 10-20 µL of RNAse-free water
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 10-20 µL of RNAse-free water.

steps.title: Measure the RNA concentration at NanoDrop spectrophotometer
steps.instruction: Measure the RNA concentration at NanoDrop spectrophotometer.

steps.title: Use 1 µg of RNA from...
steps.instruction: Use 1 µg of RNA from the previous step (the total volume shouldn't exceed 6.4 µL .

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.8 µL of DNAse buffer and 0.8 µL of DNAse I from Sigma DNAse I Kit to your RNA, if the volume is less than 8 µL , add RNAse free water until it is equal to 8 µL . Mix by pipeting. Leave for 00:20:00 on RT. (Adding 1 µL of buffer and DNAse would not result in the failure of the experiment, so one can opt to do that, if their pipette is not very precise).

steps.title: Add 1 µL of Stop solution,...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of Stop solution, mix by pipeting and put on 65 °C for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Transfer everything to a 200 μl PCR tube
steps.instruction: Transfer everything to a 200 μl PCR tube. Add 10 μl of RT buffer and 1 μl of RT enzyme from High-Capacity RNA-to-cDNA Kit. Mix by pipeting and put in the PCR machine for RT reaction. Set the program like this: 1. 37 °C for 01:00:00 2. 95 °C for 00:05:00

steps.title: Add RNAse-free water to the final volume of 200 μl
steps.instruction: Add RNAse-free water to the final volume of 200 μl. Your cDNA is ready.
